description_long: This is a vintage tabletop radio receiver with a rectangular wooden cabinet featuring rounded edges and a smooth, polished finish. The front panel displays a large horizontal speaker grille made of slatted wood, occupying the upper half, while the lower section houses two prominent round knobs and a glass-covered frequency indicator with metallic trim. The sides and top are seamless, showcasing the wood grain, and the back panel is made of a dark, perforated material for ventilation, with technical markings and a schematic diagram. The radio sits on four small feet, slightly elevating it from the surface. The overall design is classic mid-20th century, blending functionality with decorative appeal.
description: A vintage wooden tabletop radio receiver with a slatted speaker grille, two control knobs, and a glass frequency display. The cabinet is smooth and polished, with a dark ventilated back panel.
description_view_0: Front view showing the slatted speaker grille, two round control knobs, and a glass frequency indicator.
description_view_1: Left side view displaying the smooth, curved wooden surface and a small metallic handle or latch near the base.
description_view_2: Rear view featuring a dark, perforated back panel with technical markings and a schematic diagram.
description_view_3: Right side view showing the uninterrupted, polished wooden surface with no visible controls or features.

license_info.creator_display_name: Museum of Engineering and Technology, Krakow
